title: lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64 returns mutable alias to internal state
description:
lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() returns const uint8_t* to lora_state.eui64, an internal static buffer. While the const qualifier discourages mutation, C allows callers to cast away const. The header doc says 'Do NOT modify' but this is not enforced.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:366-373
Header: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.h:126-132

What goes wrong: A caller could cast away const and modify the EUI-64, corrupting mesh identity. All subsequent packets would use the corrupted address. Since EUI-64 is also used for IPv6 IID derivation (iface_link_addr copy in lichen_l2.c:388), mesh routing would break silently.

Mitigation options:
1. Accept the risk - const is sufficient documentation for cooperative callers
2. Return a copy into caller-provided buffer (like lichen_eui64_to_iid pattern)

This is a design smell rather than an exploitable bug in the current codebase since all current callers respect const. Filing as task/priority-2.
